Controls the degree of referent removal during generation. S1 anonymized (default) and S2 category-level are implemented; S3 invented lexicon is deferred and has since been reconceived as a central mechanism, not just a stricter rung.

Severity sets how aggressively generation and filtering remove referents. S1 forbids proper nouns, numbers, dates, and named real entities while allowing generic common nouns. S2 additionally prefers category-level terms over specific kinds. S3, a global invented lexicon, is now understood as the persistent world-state mechanism (node 36), tracked separately.

Severity is the dial on the degree of knowledge absence. It simultaneously shapes the generation system prompt and the strictness of the leakage filter. S1 keeps generic nouns such as forest, lake, animal, stone. S2 pushes toward generic categories such as a creature, a substance, a structure, a body of water. S3 was originally scoped as only a stricter vocabulary rung; it has since been reconceived as the persistent world-state generator (node 36), which also supplies corpus-wide consistency and information content, not just an invented word list. S1 also currently over-strips ordinary quantity grammar along with real-world numbers; see the abstract counting domain (node 40) for the proposed fix.
